lines: A work-tally nailed above a row chained at the wrist. The bones are still set in the posture of labor, propped where they dropped.
'The road-takers and the knife-men. The diocese sent them here instead of the rope, and I made them useful first. They cut these galleries. They carried their own dead into the walls.'
'When a man stopped being able to work, I stopped feeding him, and the litany did the rest. I am not ashamed of this row. They earned their cold. That is more than most down here can say.'
